---
title: "In-Class Exercise 1"
author: "Yee Weicong Mark"
date: "April 18, 2026"
date-modified: "last-modified"
execute: 
  eval: true
  echo: true
  warning: false
  freeze: true
format:
  html:
    css: styles.css
---

## [Tableau Desktop]{style="color:  #4682B4; font-size: 38px;"}

:::: {style="display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;"}
![](images/clipboard-667902076.png){width="243"}

::: {style="flex: 1;"}
Tableau is a visual analytics authoring tool that enables users to connect to data sources (Excel, CSV, databases, cloud services), explore them through drag-and-drop interactions, and build interactive visualisations and dashboards without writing code.
:::
::::

Tableau Desktop connects to Excel files directly through its **Connect** pane. *Superstore* excel file was imported.

![](images/clipboard-1402989831.png)

A **sheet** in Tableau is a single visualisation workspace. Sheets from *Superstore* were explored.

![](images/clipboard-53065662.png)
